body.title: AnnounceIdleLoop — IMAP IDLE-driven push wakeup wrapping announce_pump
body.result: AnnounceIdleLoop ships: IMAP IDLE daemon thread runs callback on EXISTS, startup-catchup pump, clean stop via direct sock.shutdown. Bonus: stub IDLE handler now exits cleanly on peer disconnect (previously blocked asyncio loop shutdown). 5 new tests; 210 passed. agent_datacenter commit db54d6d.
body.description:
Today Skeleton.announce_pump() is called manually (e.g. test pumper threads, slice-3b Invalidator already polls). For production the broker should wake on IMAP IDLE EXISTS notifications instead of polling — when an agent posts to comms://announce, the broker pumps within milliseconds. Slice 3b ships AnnounceIdleLoop: a background thread that holds an imaplib IDLE connection on comms://announce, exits IDLE on EXISTS, calls a configured callable (e.g. skeleton.announce_pump), then re-enters IDLE. Clean shutdown closes the socket so readline() returns and the loop exits.

**Affected files:** /home/akien/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/announce/idle_loop.py (NEW — AnnounceIdleLoop class), /home/akien/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/announce/__init__.py (export), /home/akien/dev/src/agent_datacenter/tests/test_idle_loop.py (NEW)
**Design rules:** holds its own imaplib connection (does NOT share with IMAPServer's internal client); shutdown closes socket from outer thread to unblock readline() — no signal/select trickery; reconnect-on-disconnect is OUT of scope (slice 3b-2 — single-shot loop is fine for v1).
**Scope boundary:** in — AnnounceIdleLoop class, start/stop, callback fires on EXISTS, clean socket close on stop, integration test that verifies push-wake within 1s. Out — auto-reconnect on connection drop, multi-mailbox watching, integration into Skeleton.__init__ (caller wires it explicitly).
**Test plan:** test_idle_loop_wakes_callback_on_append (start loop, append envelope, callback called within 1s); test_stop_unblocks_idle_cleanly (start loop on empty mailbox, stop, thread exits within 1s); test_callback_exception_does_not_kill_loop (callback raises, loop continues to wake on next EXISTS); test_double_start_is_idempotent.
